entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Open Payment Framework -Lower Costs, Quicker Deployments and Greater Flexibility
entry.content:
Overview

Integrating SAP Commerce Cloud with a payment service provider can be challenging for several reasons. The integration is highly proprietary and requires custom code, which results in higher development and maintenance costs. It may take longer to integrate a new payment service provider or expand into new regions. Open Payment Framework (OPF) is developed and released to address the challenges.

Let’s look at the Key challenges and pain points for the ecommerce business in payments perspective 

Key challenges and pain points associated with traditional payment service provider implementations: effectively addressed by the Open Payment Framework.

  • Integration Complexity:  Traditional payment integration requires individual configuration points between various commerce and payment service providers, it adds custom development burden (need to code, integrate, and deploy extensions to the Commerce Cloud codebase)
  • Scalability: Implementation restricted to specific payment providers due to custom code, making it challenging and costly to switch between PSPs (Each implementation costs months to develop, test and deploy)
  • PCI Compliance:  Every PSP implementation needs to maintain PCI DSS compliance also need to eliminate the risk of storing critical credit card data
  • Limitations: Limitations of being restricted to only credit card payments, enabling support for alternative payment methods like PayPal, Alipay, and Buy Now Pay Later may need additional options and effort
  • Regional Payment Constraints:  Retailers need to pick payment partners based on unique requirements across various markets and regions

Open Payment Framework (OPF) – What Is It?

The Open Payment Framework (OPF) is a cloud-based service within SAP Commerce Cloud that provides a centralized, plug-and-play solution for integrating digital payment service providers. Through this central hub, SAP Commerce Cloud and payment service providers (PSPs) can communicate more easily and efficiently, eliminating custom coding and complex integrations that can be expensive to build and maintain.

  • SAP Commerce Cloud's open payment framework allows you to integrate your preferred digital Payment Service Provider (PSP) intuitively
  • It is available for all SAP Commerce cloud customers as part of existing contract without any additional cost.
  • Enables BYOG (Bring your own gateway) with low Code /No Code integration with Payment Service Providers
  • Provides prebuilt Accelerators serving as reference templates for payment gateways like Adyen, Stripe, Worldpay, Airwallex, Checkout.com and many more across regions
  • Delivers a Composable Storefront (Spartacus) Extension to enable Merchants to quickly integrate Payment UIs (also provide service support for accelerator-based storefront, third party JS storefront)  
  • This platform offers a single, consolidated view of payment transactions from all service providers, along with problem diagnostic tooling like Request and Notification Logs
  • OPF can coexist with existing legacy payment integrations which are implemented already
  • PSP configuration effort - Using existing sample template, it will be less taking hours and PSP configuration from scratch (new payment gateway which is not onboarded in OPF) will take 1-3 days

 

Business Benefits of Open Payment Framework (OPF) implementation

  • No Additional Cost: Available for all SAP Commerce cloud customers as part of existing contract without any additional cost
  • Accelerated Integration: Prebuilt, standardized connections eliminate custom integrations, significantly reducing deployment time for new payment methods and providers
  • Broder Coverage: Support for payment providers and support to alternate payment methods across regions
  • Centralized Management: Provides unified payment integration, logging, and management across all payment service providers in a single interface
  • Automated Reconciliation: Eliminates manual effort in post-processing bank statements through automated reconciliation of payments with open items
  • Reduced Development and operation Costs: Low-code/no-code interface minimizes development resources required for payment integration
  • Enhanced Customer Experience: Provides customers with diverse digital payment options while ensuring smooth end-to-end payment experiences
  • Market Agility: Enables businesses to adapt quickly to changing market dynamics and customer preferences through flexible payment provider switching
  • Enhanced Security: Tokenization approach ensures PCI compliance while maintaining secure payment processing - PCI DSS 4.0 AOC certification.
  • Comprehensive Transaction Support: Supports all traditional payment methods including payment authorization, capture, refund, cancellation, and provides unified transaction visibility  
  • Flexible UX Patterns: Supports multiple payment form patterns including hosted pages, iframe integration, and hosted fields for maximum flexibility

Storefronts communicate with the open payment framework (OPF) via REST Web services.

OPF processes the requests from the consumer applications and routes them to the relevant external payment service provider (PSP). It contains one adapter for connecting the open payment framework with SAP Commerce Cloud. The open payment framework core service and the open payment framework workbench enable the connection to external PSPs.

The open payment framework and the external PSPs communicate using Web services. Most of this communication is synchronous.

SAP Commerce Cloud - Open payment framework usage guidance

OPF can be implemented for new / existing Commerce Cloud (2211.35 and above) customers. Existing customers may use OPF to add new payment providers while retaining the existing legacy implementation or they can migrate entirely to OPF as single solution

OPF supports SAP Commerce Composable Storefront with out of the box also support the accelerator storefront with sample code, it also supports third party storefront using OPF JS library  

Resources to refer

  • Early Adopter Care Registration
  • Training- OPF Microlearning Videos
  • Onboarding guide
  • Help center documentation
  • Demo Video - SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework Overview - SAP Media Share
  • Commerce Cloud Services Detail for OPF

Call-to-Action Suggestion

Explore how your organization can leverage SAP Commerce Cloud Open Payment Framework to accelerate payment innovation and reduce integration complexity in your digital commerce strategy. Please contact your SAP representative for more information 

Thanks much for your time, hope the information is helpful. All The best!

entry.title: Enhancing Customer Data Accuracy with SAP DQM Address Validation in CX Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Why Address Quality Matters in Customer Experience

In today’s customer-centric world, accurate address data is far more than a backend requirement — it directly impacts customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and compliance. Invalid or inconsistent addresses lead to failed deliveries, delayed services, poor agent productivity, and frustrated customers.

With SAP CX Service Cloud V2, organizations can significantly improve data quality by integrating SAP Data Quality Management (DQM) – Address Validation Service, ensuring that customer addresses are accurate, standardized, and validated in real time.

What is SAP DQM – Address Validation?

SAP DQM Address Validation is a cloud-based service that helps organizations:

  • Validate postal addresses in real time
  • Standardize address formats based on country-specific rules
  • Enrich address data with missing components
  • Reduce duplicates and incorrect records

The service supports global address validation across multiple countries, making it ideal for enterprises operating at scale.

Why Integrate DQM with CX Service Cloud V2?

CX Service Cloud V2 acts as the single source of truth for customer interactions. Ensuring clean address data at this layer brings several benefits:

Key Business Benefits

  • 🚀Faster case handling with fewer address-related corrections
  • 📦Reduced delivery failures and service disruptions
  • 😊Improved customer trust and experience
  • 📊Better reporting, analytics, and segmentation

Key Technical Benefits

  • Real-time validation during data entry
  • API-based integration aligned with SAP BTP standards
  • Reusable validation logic across multiple CX touchpoints

Prerequisites

Before enabling the DQM service, ensure the following are in place:

  • SAP BTP account (Cloud Foundry environment)
  • Entitlement to SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data
  • CX Service Cloud V2 tenant
  • Basic understanding of SAP BTP Destinations and API consumption
  • SAP Build Apps

Enable DQM Service in SAP BTP

  • Navigate to SAP BTP Cockpit
  • Go to Entitlements → Entity Assignments
  • Add entitlement for SAP Data Quality Management
  • Create a Service Instance for Address Validation

Configure Authentication & Access

DQM services are secured using OAuth 2.0:

  • Create a Service Key for the DQM instance
  • Capture:
    • Client ID
    • Client Secret
    • Token URL
    • API endpoint

These credentials are used by CX extensions or middleware to securely call the DQM API.

Create Destination in SAP BTP

Create an HTTP destination to consume the DQM service:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • Token Service URL: From service key
  • Client ID / Secret: From service key
  • Target URL: DQM Address Validation endpoint

This destination enables secure and reusable connectivity from:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • Custom CX extensions

Similarly create for SAP Service Cloud V2

Create SAP Build Apps Project

  • Open SAP Build Apps
  • Create a new project (Web or Responsive App)
  • Choose Blank App or Master-Detail template
  • Name it: CX_Address_Validation_App
  • Design your site as per you requirement 

Create Mashup in CX Service Cloud V2

  • Log in to CX Service Cloud V2
  • Navigate to:
    Administrator → Mashup Authoring
  • Click New Mashup
  • Provide:
    • Name: Address Validation – DQM
    • Type: URL Mashup
    • URL: Build Apps deployed URL

✔ Save the mashup.

Embedded Mashup in Account - CX Service Cloud V2

For a detailed walkthrough of the functionality, please refer to the video.
It showcases the embedded address validation experience in CX Service Cloud V2.

entry.title: SAP Enhancement Request – Mass Deletion of Parked Documents (Workflow)
entry.content:
Business Background

Currently, parked documents created through workflow cannot be deleted in bulk. Each document must be deleted manually, which is time‑consuming and operationally inefficient, especially when workflows fail, are cancelled, or remain stuck.

This issue impacts period-end processing, data cleanup, and user productivity.

Current Limitation

  • Parked documents created via workflow:
    • Cannot be deleted using existing standard SAP mass transactions
    • Require manual deletion one document at a time
  • No standard tool is available to:
    • Select parked documents by criteria (date, user, workflow ID, company code, etc.)
    • Perform controlled mass deletion

Business Requirement

Request SAP to provide an enhancement or solution that allows mass deletion of parked documents created through workflow, with appropriate controls and authorization checks.

entry.title: Re: Mapping custom retention field into the supplier invoice
entry.content:
Good day @sans16 

Thank you for your questions.

Your question touches two different topics, namely:

(A)Printing retention on the PO output and

(B) how retention behaves in Supplier Invoice (Payment block/special G/L).

So, let’s look into each:

(A)

The reason why retention prints only after you edit the PO again is because, in New Ouput Management, the system creates the PO output from an output snapshot. If your retention value is filled late (for example via a custom logic after the system initially saves the snapshot), the system may generate the first output item before your value is available. This means you only see it after a second change save triggers a new output scenario. KBA 2685826 and 3008187 describe that use case.

If you already moved retention into a custom field for printing, do not fill it when you save PO. Do this during output processing with the output BadIs (MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT  and MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_ITEM_EXT (released).

(B)

Linking your custom retention field to Supplier Invoice retention logic will require adhering to the defined logic. Which is:

  • Retention is maintained on the PO header or item level.
  • The system transfers the retention data as default values to logistics invoice verification. Which is in this case the supplier invoice.
  • Then Supplier Invoice retention amount, payment blocking and posting for retains are calculated from the standard retention fields, not from customer custom fields. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/676fb6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html for more details.

So, even if your custom field prints correctly, the retention calculation for your Supplier Invoice will not read it. The public cloud does not provide a BAdI to replace the standard process.

Regarding “Special G/L” point you made, if your goal is to drive payment block and to post to a specific G/L indicator, note that the public cloud special G/L indicators are not customisable through SSCUI. This is described in KBA 2761212

Suggestion / Summary:

  1. I suggest you use standard retention fields in the PO so the value flow automatically into the Supplier Invoice retention calculation
  2. Use the suggested PO Output BadIs so retention is present in the form when you first generate an output.
  3. If you need a different behaviour when the system posts retention offers, then that will be a process design question for you. The reason is you cannot re wire standard retention calculation to a custom field in Public Cloud.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2026-02-02?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community and Happy New Year! 🎉

Welcome to the first 2026 edition of the “What’s New” series for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM). As we kick off the year, we’re excited to share a few updates focused on strengthening operational visibility, improving the user experience, and supporting smoother collaboration across teams.

Let’s dive into what’s new! 😊

User Settings | User Profile

Great news! We’re introducing a new User Profile entry point in the application header to make user-related actions easier to find and faster to access. This enhancement is designed for signed-in users and provides a centralized place to review account details, adjust display preferences, manage notifications, and quickly access application information

With this update, users can open the User Profile (top-right) and access three options:

  • User Preferences
  • About
  • Sign Out

Previously, these options were accessible from the hamburger menu (left-side navigation). They are now moved to the User Profile (top-right) to keep navigation focused on application areas, while user actions remain consistently available in the header.

Choosing User Preferences opens a dedicated dialog titled “User Preferences”, with three sections on the left for quick navigation:

  • Account

  • Language and Format

  • Notifications

This keeps frequently used personalization settings in one place and reduces the need to navigate across different areas of the UI.

The About option opens a dialog titled “About This Application”

Choosing Sign Out logs the user out of the application.

Settings Screen | Destination Settings: SAP Cloud ALM

A new enhancement has been introduced in the Destinations Settings section under the SAP Cloud ALM option. When changes are made to the SAP Cloud ALM destination configuration and saved, an exception log is automatically sent to SAP Cloud ALM within the Integration and Exception Monitoring. This ensures full traceability of the changes, faster troubleshooting, and improved audit trail compliance for destination changes.

Modeling | Runtime Attributes (Stored Procedure Viewer)

When a modeler activates a function, SAP PaPM generates runtime artifacts (such as tables and stored procedures) that support execution.

In some cases, activation can fail, and the stored procedure may not be available in the database making it harder to understand what went wrong.

With this enhancement, modelers can now view the generated stored procedure source code directly in the Modeling UI under Runtime Attributes, helping them troubleshoot and identify modeling issues more easily even when activation is not successful.

What’s improved

  • More self-service troubleshooting: Quickly check the generated procedure text to understand activation problems and adjust the model accordingly.
  • On-demand loading: The procedure source is fetched only when the modeler opens/views it, keeping the Modeling screen responsive.
  • Secure approach: Designed to support debugging without exposing procedure source code through general logging for broader audiences.

Where it’s available

This viewer is available under Runtime Attributes for these function types:

  • Allocation
  • Calculation
  • Conversion
  • Derivation
  • Transfer Structure
  • Join
  • View

How to use it

  1. Go to Modeling and select a supported function
  2. Activate the supported function if not yet activated
  3. In the Properties Panel, choose the drop-down arrow of Runtime Attributes.
  4. Expand Stored Procedure to load and display the stored procedure text

Successful activation:

Failed activation:

Teams | In-App Notification

An enhancement for notifications in the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model is now available.  If the user has notifications enabled, they will receive the new team-related notifications by email, in-app, or both.

Users will be alerted in the following scenarios:

  • When they are assigned to or unassigned from a team
  • When their team is added or removed as a child team
  • When their team’s name or description is updated
  • When their team becomes obsolete, or is restored from an obsolete status
  • When their team is deleted

See sample image below:

To learn more about notification settings, please refer to the Notifications | SAP Help Portal

What’s New on SAP Help Portal

We’re excited to share that SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud now has a dedicated What’s New section on the SAP Help Portal. This new page complements the existing What’s New blog posts in the SAP Community and provides a central, structured view of all product updates.

The What’s New page offers a clear overview of the latest features, enhancements, and content updates across the Standard Model, Universal Model, and Sample Content. Updated in alignment with the product delivery schedule, it gives customers a simple, reliable way to stay informed about ongoing improvements in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud.

How to View Standard Model–Specific Updates

To display only Standard Model–related information in the What’s New table, follow these steps:

  1. Open the What's New to SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud page on the SAP Help Portal.
  2. Under Release Type, select one of the following options:
    1. Standard Model – shows updates for the default release cadence mode
    2. Standard Model Fast Track – shows updates for the Fast Track cadence mode
  3. (Optional) Under Scope, refine your view:
    1. Application – updates relevant for all users
    2. Sample Content – updates related to Sample Content
    3. Administration – updates relevant for administrators
  4. (Optional) Under Type, filter by New, Changed, Deleted or Deprecated.
  5. (Optional) Under Available as Of, choose a specific date range to explore updates for a particular time period.

That’s a wrap! 🎁

Thank you for reading, and we hope these updates help make your day-to-day work in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model even smoother.

Stay tuned for the next edition of our “What’s New” series as we continue to roll out more enhancements throughout 2026.

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 1)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

My name is Sebastiano Marchesini, SAP Champion and SAP BTP architect. I moved from Italy to the US and currently work as a technology lead consultant, focusing on SAP BTP and enterprise architectures. I also love running 🙂

From my experience at SAP TechEd, learning sessions, and live events, the most valuable moments are always the same:

Asking questions, any kind of question, and getting direct, honest answers from experts.

For this reason, the SAP Champions are launching a new monthly AMA (Ask Me Anything) series starting in 2026.

Each month, a different SAP Champion with a specific area of expertise (BTP, architecture, integration, development, automation, career growth, and more) will answer questions from the community, both technical and non-technical.

This blog post is the first of the series.

You are invited to ask any questions you have, openly or anonymously, using the form below.
No topic is off-limits: architecture doubts, product choices, real-life SAP challenges, or even questions about working in the SAP ecosystem, running (lol).

Let’s make this a space for open discussion, real experience, and practical answers.

You can ask anonymously here


AMA Champion Anonymously Questions

(or below as anwer of the blog) 

Submit your questions by early February and get answers in our upcoming SAP Communities video and blog recap.



This AMA session will be open for questions until the last week of February.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Communities YouTube channel.
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Only questions submitted during this window will be considered.

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series: Two Hearts, Four Paws: Growing Together Through Dog Agility
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

my name is Joker and I’m a mixed-breed dog, 4 years old… woof woof… ok, I’m almost 5. I’m here to tell you about one of my favourite hobbies, besides reading books.

 Hmm… woof woof…..ok, wrong photo.

 

Much better!
This hobby is called Dog Agility Sport, and I practice it together with my best friend @patty_1982 , whom you probably already know because I believe she’s quite well known in this community… woof woof… wooof…

 

............…sorry about that, my mischievous dog just stole my keyboard 😁😂

Here I am, I’ll take over from here.

My name is Patrizia Rossi, I’m a SAP Solution Architect, I have strong expertise in SAP Business Technology Platform, and I’m currently part of the IT team at the European Space Agency (don’t worry, I’m not the one going to the moon 👩‍🚀).
As you may have guessed, I’ve had a dog for almost five years now, and he has truly changed my life. Thanks to him, I discovered that I could do so many other things, not just the usual walks in the park. Four years ago, we started this journey to learn a wonderful sport: Dog Agility Sport.
Like all sports, it requires discipline and, above all, control of your dog. This control is essential because the dog must not get distracted by the surrounding environment, such as the presence of an audience watching, noises around the field, or even other dogs nearby. Throughout the entire run, the dog needs to stay focused on the handler, maintaining attention, trust, and connection from the first obstacle to the last. In this sport, Joker and I are what is called a team (or handler–dog pair). I have to say we were lucky, because there was already an incredible mental connection between us, and that really helped us grow together in this sport.

As I mentioned before, a strong connection is essential.
As a handler, you must guide your dog through the obstacle course, and the dog must understand what to do through your voice and your gestures. The field covers a large area and is made up of obstacles such as jumps, tunnels, slalom poles, the dog walk, and many others. The number of obstacles can go up to 22, although I can assure you that during training, we easily reach 28 😅. What you need, in addition to physical training, is a very good memory… and a solid dose of problem-solving skills 😎

Considering that in every training session or competition the course is always different, the first thing the handler must do is walk the course… without the four-legged friend. 🐕
You need to walk the field to understand the route the dog will have to follow and memorize it. During competitions, you don’t have many minutes to do this.

There are numbers that indicate the sequence of the obstacles, but they don’t explain how the dog should approach them: whether to turn around, enter from the front, from the back, and so on. That’s something you have to figure out yourself, by literally putting yourself in your dog’s paws. 😮 Yes, exactly: the point of view is the dog’s, not yours. Based on that, you must decide which voice commands or gestures to use.

Our training day is Friday evening: a wonderful way to end the working week and completely switch off. Nothing stops us neither rain, nor winter cold, nor summer heat.
This sport makes both of us feel good, because our main goal is simply to have fun. How do you teach a dog this sport? You need a lot of patience and some tasty snacks or appealing rewards. With dogs like Joker, training is done through shaping.

A bit like machine learning: step by step, the dog learns the mechanisms, and over time it takes very little for him to understand what to do. Very often, he even anticipates me! We are also very aware of our limits: Joker is a mixed-breed dog and will never be as lightning-fast as a Border Collie. But he’s truly great and learns very quickly. For example, it took some time for him to get used to the seesaw, because at the beginning he was scared by the noise the plank makes when it hits the ground during the descent.

That said, training is not only about teaching the dog, I also need constant learning and guidance. That’s why we work with a trainer, Elisa 😊, who helps us improve as a team. One of the most important aspects for the handler is understanding the right moment to indicate an obstacle to the dog, as well as maintaining correct body posture throughout the run.

Yes, that’s right: our body speaks to the dog far more than we often realize. If an obstacle is straight ahead but our feet are pointing in a different direction, the dog will take the wrong line. Or, if we open our arm to indicate a jump but fail to properly open our chest, the dog may interpret the signal differently and run behind the obstacle instead of jumping it. In agility, timing, posture, and body language are just as important as speed.
And yes, friends 😅 now imagine having to do all of this while running at full speed and, at the same time, trying to remember every single step of the course.

Our “flaw”, if you can call it that, is that we are so closely bonded that we can’t stay far apart. If I must send him into a tunnel from a distance, I can’t stay too far away, because he always wants me nearby 😉
This means I have to run more on the field, to stay as close as possible to the obstacles with him.
What I can tell you, though, is that all of this is rewarded in an invaluable way: when your dog looks straight into your eyes and shows you complete trust in being guided through this wonderful adventure.

 

 

 

Every now and then we also take part in competitions, but as I mentioned at the beginning, our goal has never been about trophies or podiums. For us, competitions are part of the sport: a way to test our training, to read a new course, to manage emotions, and to grow as a handler–dog team under pressure.
Our real success lies in the run itself: in every clear obstacle, in every decision made in a split second, in the trust that allows my dog to follow my lead without hesitation. It’s about stepping off the field tired but fulfilled, knowing we gave our best, learned something new, and strengthened our connection once again. And when, at the end of the day, we are already looking forward to training again the following Friday, that’s when we truly know we’re winning.

entry.title: Step by step procedure to integrate S/4 HANA On-Premise system with GitHub
entry.content:
Introduction:

For every developer, it is essential to write code following best practices, stay updated with new syntax, and focus on reducing data latency to achieve optimal performance. Equally important is improving hands-on experience by consistently practicing coding exercises.

While working in SAP test systems, ABAP objects created for practice are usually stored temporarily. These objects are removed once system access is expired or revoked. As a result, developers often resort to copying code into a notepad or taking screenshots for future reference, which is neither efficient nor scalable.

To overcome this limitation, it is advisable to store ABAP objects in a personal GitHub repository using the Push operation. When access to the test or sandbox system is revoked, the same objects can be retrieved into a new SAP test system using the Pull operation from the repository.

With this objective, this blog explains how to integrate an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system with GitHub.

Steps to follow to integrate S/4 HANA On-Premise system with GitHub:

Step 1: Install abapGit in S/4HANA

  • Go to the URL https://abapgit.org
  • Select GitHub option to obtain installer code from GitHub READ.ME
  • Select the latest installer code from zabapgit_source.abap file which is included in the READ.ME.
  • Copy the latest installer code.
  • In SAP GUI, go to transaction SE38
  • Create program: ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE
  • Paste the code which is copied from installer file.

Download this file into the local system.

Step 2: Create GitHub repository

  • Create a personal GitHub repository in the github.com to push practiced ABAP objects from S/4 HANA system to the GitHub repo. Prerequisite here is to have a GitHub personal account. 

Step 3: Generate GitHub Personal Token Access (PAT):

PAT acts like a password for the GitHub username while connecting to the GitHub repository in the SAP system.

  • Go to Settings-->Developer settings-->PAT-->Tokens(classic).
  • Save the generated token for the authentication purpose from SAP. It will be visible only once after its generation. Therefore, it is better to save it on Notepad or others.

STEP 4: Maintain SSL Certificates in SAP

GitHub uses HTTPS → SSL trust needed.

Steps:

  1. Go to transaction STRUST and select SSL Client (Anonymous / Standard)
  2. Import GitHub root certificates.
  3. Save & activate.
  4. Reload ICM in the SMICM transaction.

Before Importing GitHub certificate, it must be exported from GitHub URL.

Export GitHub Root certificates:

  • Export GitHub Repository certificate from Github.
  • Go to GitHub URL and click on the toggle symbol which is at the starting of the URL.
  • Now select “Connection is secure” option.
  • Click on “Certificate is valid” option.
  • Certificate details will be opened. Select the Certificate Hierarchy and click on Export.
  • File will be exported into the local system (Presentation layer)

Import GitHub certificate: 

Now import the certificate from the local system.

  • Go to STRUST transaction.
  • Select SSL Client (Anonymous).
  • Click on Certificate option in the menu bar and click on Import option.
  • Select the downloaded GitHub file from the local system.
  • Click on continue.
  • Now certificate will be added in the STRUST.
  • Go to SMICM transaction.
  • Select Administration option from the menu bar.
  • Select ICM-->Configuration-->Click on Reload.
  • Certificate will be visible in the system after this step. 

Step 5: Create Online Repository using abapGit

  • Execute the newly created program “ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE” in SE38.
  • Click on New Online Repository.
  • Provide GitHub Repository URL.
  • Select the custom package from SAP where practice objects are stored.
  • Provide Label details.
  • Click on Create Online Repo.
  • Entry will be added after this step.

Step 6: Push/Pull objects from S/4 HANA system to GitHub Repo & Vice-versa

  • Click on Stage to push all the objects from SAP package to the provided GitHub repository.
  • Pull the objects from GitHub Repo to SAP if required.
  • All the objects are now visible in the GitHub Repo.
  • Same can be verified in the GitHub Repo.

In the similar way, multiple repositories can be added in the abapGit by following above mentioned steps.

Conclusion:

Therefore, Integrating an S/4HANA on-premise system with GitHub using abapGit is the best practice for ABAP developers, especially when working in temporary or learning environments. It ensures code preservation, promotes structured learning, and reduces dependency on system availability. By following a clear setup process, developers can seamlessly push and pull ABAP objects, making GitHub a reliable companion for continuous ABAP skill development.

Note:  Intension of this blog is to follow this procedure while using test systems only as this is written entirely from the test systems perspective. 

#abapGit

#S/4 HANA On-Premise

#GitHub

#ABAP

entry.title: Free Learning Journeys for Learning WalkMe which leads to certification
entry.content:
SAP WalkMe helps organizations to effectively implement their digital adoption strategies to increase the effectiveness and productiveness of their digital transformation journey. WalkMe is a digital adoption platform providing customers with framework which makes the Digital Transformation easier, faster and simpler. 

SAP Learning offers two learning journeys for learners:

  1. Positioning WalkMe

    This learning journey aims to provide you with core skills and knowledge of WalkMe and expertise to effectively discover, position, sell and recommend D.A.P in the market. The learning journey comprises of short courses - each about an hour long to consume. You will also be able to develop a deep understanding of WalkMe’s five key differentiators to effectively communicate its ROI, productivity benefits, and innovation leadership, apply the “Why Change, Why Now, Why WalkMe” framework to address customer concerns and emphasize the urgency of digital adoption and analyze the competitive landscape to confidently position WalkMe as the ultimate solution for enterprise digital transformation and long-term success.

    This learning journey is ideal for learners who are:
    1. In active customer-facing roles,
    2. Who are involved in digital transformation & Business Transformation Management roles
    3. Someone who are trying to understand the overview & basic features of WalkMe 

      Access the learning journey using this link here.

      Please note that completion of each course within the learning journey will entitle you for a completion badge. 

  2. Starting your Digital adoption journey with WalkMe

    This learning journey is the certification path for getting  certified as SAP Certified - WalkMe Digital Adoption Consultant. This certification verifies that the recipient has acquired a foundational understanding of Digital Adoption and the WalkMe platform, along with a comprehensive grasp of how to build solutions using WalkMe. Participants will learn to define key concepts, appreciate the value and applications of WalkMe, understand WalkMe's success methodology, and apply this knowledge to create content that addresses business challenges effectively.  

    This learning journey comprises of 3 courses - a) WalkMe Fundamentals, b) Getting Started with Building WalkMe Solutions and c) Advancing your skills in building WalkMe solutions. While fundamentals is a basic course covering high level details, the other two courses delve deeper into the WalkMe product to provide a detailed overview of concepts, tools, procedures, best practices etc. 

    This learning journey is ideal for learners who are:
    1. Involved in strategizing, designing and delivering digital adoption platform 
    2. Actively involved in WalkMe administration, Content creation, Analysis of user experience managemen
    3. Interested in getting WalkMe certification

      Access the learning journey using this link here.

      Please note that completion of each course within the learning journey will entitle you for a completion badge.

We also have a Get Certified Live session coming in March 2026 - you can get more details here about the same and also register.

Let’s be honest, certifications can feel overwhelming.  You know the platform.  You’ve touched the systems.  Still, the exam looms, and the question pops up: Am I ready?

The Partner Learning Accelerator: Certified Academy for SAP BTP Administrator was built for that exact moment.

In this 6-hour live session, we slow things down just enough to make everything click.  From SAP BTP environment administration to security, identity services, and cloud operations, we connect real-world partner experience with what the certification actually expects.

The thing is this isn’t about cramming.  It’s about understanding.  About walking away thinking, “Okay—now this makes sense”.

If you’ve already worked with SAP BTP and completed the recommended learning journey, this academy can be the bridge between hands-on work and exam success.

Join us, sharpen your skills, and take that next step with confidence.

entry.title: Integrating External Exchange Rate API with SAP SuccessFactors for Training MDF Currency Conversions
entry.content:
Introduction :

One of my Clients has been using Training Custom MDF for nearly a year now and they always found the currency conversion the hardest , going through each requests , converting the cross -currency to their native currency. You can also ask the employees to fill in the Currency Conversion Rates but that's always not the best way out , This is a  Gap in the Process which needs to be addressed and with Intelligent System like the SAP SuccessFactors this should be easy.

Lets Break down the Configuration into Pieces :

Before we deep dive , Lets get the Scope Defined!

  1. Currency Exchange Rate MDF
  2. Exchange Rate API
  3. Integration Flow to Automate the Import Process
  4. Lookup Rule
  5. Currency Conversion 

Technical Architecture : 

Currency Exchange Rate MDF:

SAP SuccessFactors has provided a Generic Object named 'Currency Exchange Rate' which is the source of data for the Currency Conversion. The Object consists of the following fields

  1. Currency Exchange Rate Type which should always be set to DEFAULT
  2. Source Currency
  3. Target Curency
  4. Exchange Rate

\

(Screenshot of the Sample Data from Manage Data -> Currency Exchange Rate)

For details on the Currency Exchange Rate MDF , Please checkout the link below :

Setting Up the Currency Exchange Rate | SAP Help Portal

Lets get to know the most interesting part of the blog!

Nobody wants to do manual work everyday (updating Currency Exchange Rates) for each target currency. That's where things get little techincal and interesting!

I wanted to automate this process End-to-End ensuring seamless automation and what sparked is the below

Automation of the Currency Exchange Rates using API : 

There are multiple open source Exchange Rate APIs out there , the one I prefer and recommend is the  ExchangeRate-API which is reliable and Quick to Setup .Attaching the link beow

ExchangeRate-API - Free & Pro Currency Converter API

The Billing Plan is free and gives access to 1500 API calls / Month and the update is every 24 hours. If you need faster and real time currency updates, you can consider subscribing to their Paid Plans. The API endpoint is so simple and standard.

Sample End Point :

https://v6.exchangerate-api.com/v6/YOUR-API-KEY/latest/Currency

Replace the YOUR-API-KEY with your unique key displayed in the Dasboard and Currency should be replaced by your desired Three-Digit currency code.

Sample API Response:

 

 "result":"success",
 "documentation":"https://www.exchangerate-api.com/docs",
 "terms_of_use":"https://www.exchangerate-api.com/terms",
 "time_last_update_unix":1769731201,
 "time_last_update_utc":"Fri, 30 Jan 2026 00:00:01 +0000",
 "time_next_update_unix":1769817601,
 "time_next_update_utc":"Sat, 31 Jan 2026 00:00:01 +0000",
 "base_code":"USD",
 "conversion_rates":{
  "USD":1,
  "AED":3.6725,
  "AFN":65.3799,
  "ALL":80.7961,
  "AMD":379.0431,
  "ANG":1.7900,
  "AOA":919.4031,
  "ARS":1452.2500,
  "AUD":1.4186
}
}

 

Integration iFlow:

The most exciting and technical part of the blog is building the Integration Flow to automate the Currency Exchange Rate Updation Process.

Here is the Step by Step Guide to Build the iFlow:

Scheduler:

Scheduler is the start event for the integration flow. I have scheduled it to run once daily based on my preferrable timing. You can schedule it once, multiple based on your requirement.

Request Reply:

Request Reply is used to make an API Call to fetch the Currency Exchange Rate (GET).Add a Receiver and the adapter type is HTTP.

JSON to XML Converter:

JSON to XML Converter is used to convert the API response from JSON to XML format to upsert the records to SuccessFactors.

XLST Mapping:

The most crucial element of the integration Flow is the XLST Mapping which is basically to transform the converted XML from one format to another. Let me tell why this is need , the converted JSON TO XML Response looks like this

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<result>success</result>
<documentation>https://www.exchangerate-api.com/docs</documentation>
<terms_of_use>https://www.exchangerate-api.com/terms</terms_of_use>
<time_last_update_unix>1769731201</time_last_update_unix>
<time_last_update_utc>Fri, 30 Jan 2026 00:00:01 +0000</time_last_update_utc>
<time_next_update_unix>1769817601</time_next_update_unix>
<time_next_update_utc>Sat, 31 Jan 2026 00:00:01 +0000</time_next_update_utc>
<base_code>USD</base_code>
<conversion_rates>
<USD>1</USD>
<AED>3.6725</AED>
<AFN>65.3799</AFN>
<ALL>80.7961</ALL>
<AMD>379.0431</AMD>
</conversion_rates>

 

This will not serve our purpose as all the currency exchange rates are nested inside the  Target Currencies. Our  Response should like this

 

<currency>XXX<currency>
<rate>YYY</rate>

 

The Above format is necessary for us to map the Currency  to the Target currency field and Rate to the Currency Exchange Rate field of the Currency Exchange Rate MDF in SuccessFactors.

This is where XLST Mapping comes Handy. Copy paste the below in the processing section of the XLST Section.

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
                version="1.0">
    
    <!-- Identity template: copy everything by default -->
    <xsl:template match="@*|node()">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="@*|node()"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>
    
    <!-- Transform conversion_rates -->
    <xsl:template match="conversion_rates">
        <rates>
            <xsl:for-each select="*">
                <rate>
                    <currency>
                        <xsl:value-of select="name()"/>
                    </currency>
                    <value>
                        <xsl:value-of select="."/>
                    </value>
                </rate>
            </xsl:for-each>
        </rates>
    </xsl:template>
    
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

This will transform the XML into our Desired Structure.

Content Modifier:

Content Modifier is used here to set two exchange properties Base Code and Last Update Date UTC.

The reason behind setting exchange properties is explained in the next section!

Iterating Splitter :

Since our API Response has multiple Currency Exchange rates , it is necessary for us to split the response to form individual XML request payload for the upsert.

The reason for setting exchange properties is because of the iterating splitter as the base code and last update UTC data are lost during the split.

Groovy Script:

Add a Groovy Script step to the iFlow to convert the Last Update UTC data to the SuccessFactors accepted ISO Format.

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.time.Instant
import java.time.ZoneOffset
import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter

def Message processData(Message message) {

    // Get UNIX timestamp from exchange property "lastupdate"
    def unixTime = message.getProperty("lastupdate") as long

    // Convert UNIX timestamp to ISO 8601 format
    def lastupdateIso = Instant.ofEpochSecond(unixTime)
                               .atOffset(ZoneOffset.UTC)
                               .format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE_TIME)

    // Set as new exchange property
    message.setProperty("lastupdate", lastupdateIso)

    return message
}

 

XLST Mapping:

Add an another XLST Mapping to add the base code and Last Update UTC to each of the split individual XML Payload.

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
                version="1.0">

    <xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes"/>

    <!-- Parameters passed from CPI exchange properties -->
    <xsl:param name="basecode"/>
    <xsl:param name="lastupdate"/>

    <!-- Template for individual <rate> node -->
    <xsl:template match="rate">
        <root>
            <base_code>
                <xsl:value-of select="$basecode"/>
            </base_code>

            <time_last_update_unix>
                <xsl:value-of select="$lastupdate"/>
            </time_last_update_unix>

            <currency_code>
                <xsl:value-of select="currency"/>
            </currency_code>

            <value>
                <xsl:value-of select="value"/>
            </value>
        </root>
    </xsl:template>

</xsl:stylesheet>

 

Message Mapping:

The Crucial part of the iFlow is the message mapping where the XML payload is mapped to the Currency Exchange Rate MDF fields. I have added the mapping configuration below.Most of it is straight forward except the external code which is explained below

TARGETMAPPING
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/currencyExchangeRateTypeconst(
"DEFAULT")
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/exchangeRate/root/value
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/sourceCurrency/root/base_code
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/targetCurrency/root/currency_code
/CurrencyExchangeRate/root
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/root
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/effectiveStartDate/root/time_last_update_unix
/CurrencyExchangeRate/CurrencyExchangeRate/externalCodeconcat(
concat(
const(
"DEFAULT")
,/root/base_code,"-")
,/root/currency_code,"-")

If the External code is not mapped this way (DEFAULT-BASECURRENCY-TARGETCURRENCY) , multiple effective records won't be created and external code already exists (500 Server Error will be thrown).

Request Reply:

Add a Request Reply , add a Receiver and Setup Connection to the SuccessFactors and use the Method UPSERT.

 

Crential name is to be created in Security Material

End :

End Event to officially mark the end of the  Automation. Deploy and witness the Magic in Action!

Lookup Rule:

Now its time to assign the Lookup Rule to the Training MDF / Any MDF where Currency Conversion is required .

Replace the Source Currency with your Native Currency 

Currency Conversion:

Everything coming together in the MDF Portlet!

Conclusion :


Currency conversion is one of those areas in SuccessFactors that works really well once it’s set up correctly—but getting there can be painful if everything is done manually. In our case, maintaining exchange rates for Training MDF quickly became time-consuming and error-prone, especially when multiple currencies were involved.

By using the standard Currency Exchange Rate MDF along with a simple API-based integration, we were able to fully automate the exchange rate update process. The integration runs on a schedule, pulls the latest rates, transforms the data into the required format, and upserts it into SuccessFactors without any manual effort. Once deployed, it just works.

The real value of this solution is not just automation, but consistency. Exchange rates are always up to date, lookup rules behave as expected, and users no longer need to worry about entering or validating currency values. The same approach can also be reused for other MDFs where currency conversion is required, making it a scalable and reusable design.

Hopefully, this blog helps anyone facing similar challenges with currency conversion in SuccessFactors. If you’re dealing with manual exchange rate maintenance today, this is a small investment that can save a lot of effort in the long run.

Happy integrating! 🙂

entry.title: Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your 2026 updates for the Professional Services industry, here you can learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 in the area of Professional Services. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 
  • View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 
  • Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 
  • Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 
  • Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 
  • Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)
  • Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)
  • Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)
  • Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

Here you can have a look at the three top highlights of this release for the professional services industry here:

Intercompany cost allocations reconciliation review booklet (16T, 4AN) 

Reconciling intercompany costs is a common challenge that can complicate period-end closing. To provide complete transparency, we have delivered the "Intercompany Cost Allocations Reconciliation" review booklet. This powerful report gives the delivering company a comprehensive, step-by-step view of the entire resource-related intercompany billing process. It bridges the gap between the initial cost (the CO journal entry) and the internal billing document, clearly highlighting any unbilled items. With the 2602 release, the report is further enhanced to trace the process all the way to the corresponding FI journal entry on the receiving company's books, allowing you to quickly identify and close any reconciliation gaps.

 

This was also a request raised by customers on our Customer Influence Portal, which was resolved with this delivery: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/285049

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Please also have a look at Thorsten Seegers blog, he is the product manager experts of this topic.

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Next let's get to the topics we have this release in the project billing area.

Please remember that Andreas Hammerschmidt updates his detailed blog “Project Billing for Customer Projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud” with every release, so you can find a comprehensive overview of the functionality there.

 

View custom fields in the billing elements list of the Manage Project Billing app (4E9) 

To make truly informed billing decisions, you need the full context. We've enhanced the "Manage Project Billing" app to provide just that. Imagine your business uses custom fields on sales orders to track critical data like a "Project Priority Code," "Geographic Region," or a "Contractual Milestone ID." Now, you can add these fields as columns directly in your billing worklist. This simple change has a powerful impact: you can now sort your billing elements by priority to ensure high-value items are processed first, or filter by region to manage tax-specific requirements. This brings essential data to the forefront early in the process, reducing the need to cross-reference information and helping you prevent billing errors before they happen.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Enablement of time and expense billing without open billing plan item (4E9) 

Your ability to generate cash flow shouldn't be held up by administrative hurdles. To accelerate your time-to-bill, we've introduced a major flexibility enhancement for Time and Expense or Usage-Based billing. Consider a scenario where ad-hoc consulting work is completed and time is recorded, but a specific billing plan item for that exact period doesn't exist yet. Instead of waiting for a plan to be created, billing specialists can now immediately create a Billing Document Request for this work. The system intelligently handles the backend process by automatically generating a new billing plan item with a due date set to the current date. This ad-hoc billing capability, enabled via a feature toggle, ensures that you can invoice for completed work the moment it's ready, directly improving your cash flow.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Archival of Project Billing data (4E9) 

 Maintaining a lean, high-performing system is crucial for long-term operational excellence. To support this, we've expanded our data archiving capabilities. When a sales order tied to a customer project is closed and archived, the system now intelligently archives all associated Project Billing data along with it. This ensures that the complete historical record of a project is stored together in a secure, accessible archive. For system administrators, this means a reduced data footprint and better application performance. For compliance and auditing, this provides a clear and complete trail. An auditor-role user can easily view the count of archived entries, and the application log provides full transparency, confirming exactly which project billing requests were archived as part of the process.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Seamless access to Preliminary Billing Document apps on creation of Project Down Payment Request (4E9) 

A smooth workflow is a fast workflow. We've optimized the process for creating project down payment requests to eliminate unnecessary steps and keep you moving. Previously, after submitting a down payment request, the confirmation dialog would only link to the billing document app. This created a disjointed experience, forcing you to manually navigate to other apps to continue your work. With the 2602 release, the success dialog has been enhanced. It now includes direct navigation links to both the "Create Preliminary Billing Document" and "Manage Preliminary Billing Documents - Services" apps. This means you can flow from one logical task to the next without interruption, saving clicks and maintaining your focus.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Improving usability for projects managers to adjust internal projects within professional service projects (J11)

A great user experience is paramount. We've rolled out several enhancements to make project administration more intuitive and flexible. This includes new, streamlined Fiori apps like "Manage Projects – Professional Services Projects" for creating internal projects and "Manage Work Packages" for maintaining work-package demands. We've also listened to your feedback to provide more flexibility. You can now assign a resource to a project responsibility even if their work agreement starts in the future, allowing for better forward planning. Furthermore, the Project Control app now provides a complete financial picture by displaying all related Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions, giving you a single source of truth for all project commitments. This flexibility even extends to how you find information, as you can now edit certain SAP-delivered search models and publish them to your catalog, tailoring the search experience to your team's needs

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at these two entries for this topic on SAP Help Portal

 

Project staffing view for Resource Managers (6JO)

Effective resource management requires a bird's-eye view of project needs. The new "Manage Request Assignments" app delivers exactly that. It provides resource managers with a consolidated, project-centric dashboard showing all resource requests and their assignment status. Instead of digging into individual requests, you can now see the entire staffing picture for a project at a weekly or monthly level. This makes it easy to spot upcoming resource shortfalls or identify overstaffed areas at a glance. From this central view, you can directly navigate to the "Staff Resource Request" or "Manage Resource Utilization" apps to make proactive adjustments, ensuring optimal staffing across your entire project portfolio.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

For even more details, please have a look at the entry of this topic on SAP Help Portal.

 

Ability to find resources across resource organizations (6JO)

In a global or matrixed organization, talent can be anywhere. Our latest update breaks down internal silos to help you find the best person for the job, regardless of their home department. Resource Managers can now use the new "Resource Organization" filter in the "Staff Resource Requests" app. This powerful filter allows you to expand your search and view matching resources from multiple resource organizations you are authorized to access. The results will clearly display the "Resource Organization" for each candidate, empowering you to build the best possible project team by tapping into a wider, more diverse internal talent pool.

Have a look at this demo to get a better understanding of the system: 

 

Custom content in Resource Management apps (6JO)

We understand that every business has unique data needs for making staffing decisions. To accommodate this, you can now add custom sections and content to Resource Management apps using our built-in UI extensibility tools. For example, you could add a custom section to the Resource Request page to display an employee's "Security Clearance Level," "Industry Certifications," or "Language Skills." This ensures that all critical decision-making information is available in one place. This level of customization is supported by our robust framework, which includes a library of released CDS views like `I_LglCntntMCntntTypeTxtAPI01` (Document Content Type - Text), giving your developers stable, SAP-approved objects to build upon.

 

More Information

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Professional Services, please have a look at my collection blog  and also take a look at the Community page for Professional Services .

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

Find All of Our Assets   

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Guia Prático para Solução de Problemas – Workflow Flexível
entry.content:
O uso do Workflow Flexível oferece inúmeras vantagens, especialmente por viabilizar uma solução robusta e standard para os processos de aprovação de documentos no sistema. No entanto, apesar de sua abrangência e flexibilidade, sua correta operação depende de um conjunto de configurações técnicas, organizacionais e funcionais. Dessa forma, é fundamental que os profissionais envolvidos compreendam os principais pontos de atenção para lidar com eventuais inconsistências ao longo do processo.

Existem diversas ferramentas disponíveis para identificação e correção de erros de workflow. A proposta deste material não é esgotar todas as possibilidades, mas apresentar aquelas mais utilizadas na prática, auxiliando na condução eficiente das análises.

Investigação do erro

O primeiro passo é identificar qual tipo de erro está ocorrendo para, então, seguir para a resolução. Para isso, existem algumas ferramentas que podem ser utilizadas

1. SWIA: é utilizada para monitorar e administrar as instâncias de workflow, exibindo o status das tarefas ao longo do processo. Normalmente, este é o primeiro ponto a ser consultado durante a investigação, pois permite identificar em qual etapa o fluxo se encontra e se há indícios iniciais de falha

Os status mais comuns das tarefas são:

  • Ready / Pronto: tarefa disponível na caixa de aprovação do aprovador;
  • Completed / Encerrado: a ação de aprovação foi executada pelo aprovador;
  • Error / Incorreto: quando algum problema impede que a tarefa de aprovação chegue até o aprovador.

Para acessar a mensagem de erro de uma tarefa com status Incorreto, seguimos o seguinte caminho:

Em muitos casos, a SWIA fornece informações preliminares relevantes. No entanto, quando as mensagens apresentadas não são suficientes para identificar a causa raiz, é necessário complementar a análise com outras ferramentas.

2. SWI2_DIAG: é utilizada para diagnosticar workflows que se encontram em status de erro. Por meio dela, é possível analisar logs técnicos detalhados, identificar falhas de processamento e, quando aplicável, reprocessar ou reiniciar o fluxo após a correção das inconsistências.

3. PFAC: permite a validação das regras de determinação de agentes. Nela, é possível simular se uma determinada regra funciona corretamente para um usuário específico, evitando a necessidade de reiniciar múltiplas vezes o workflow ou gerar documentos apenas para testes.

Essa funcionalidade é fundamental para diferenciar falhas técnicas de problemas relacionados à estrutura organizacional ou às regras de aprovação.

4. SWELS: inicia ou desliga o trace/rastreamento de eventos, pode ser ativado para um usuário específico;

5. SWEL: exibe o histórico de rastreamento de eventos

Essas ferramentas são especialmente úteis quando há suspeita de que o workflow não esteja sendo iniciado corretamente.

6. SWPR: é utilizada para reiniciar uma tarefa de Workflow após a correção do erro

 

 

Resolução dos erros

Após a identificação da causa, é possível direcionar as ações corretivas de forma mais objetiva. A seguir, são apresentados alguns dos cenários mais recorrentes na operação.

 

1. Falha na determinação do agente aprovador

Quando estamos lidando com erros relacionados à determinação do agente aprovador, existem algumas possibilidades a serem consideradas. Esse tipo de falha normalmente indica um problema na leitura da estrutura de aprovação pelo sistema.

As principais causas são:

  • O usuário que iniciou o Workflow não possui business user (verificar na transação SU01D) ou não está corretamente atribuído na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades. Com isso, o sistema não consegue identificar quem está hierarquicamente acima na estrutura de aprovação;
  • O usuário aprovador não possui business user ou não está atribuído corretamente na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades;
  • O aprovador possui um substituto ativo atribuído que não possui business user ou não está corretamente vinculado na estrutura do HCM ou em Times e Responsabilidades. Nesse caso, recomenda-se verificar a existência de substitutos na transação RMPS_SET_SUBSTITUTE.

 

2. Workflow não aparece na SWIA

Quando o Workflow não aparece nas atividades da SWIA, isso pode indicar alguma falha de configuração no sistema.

Nesses casos, recomenda-se verificar:

  • No customizing do documento que está sendo criado, se a ativação do Workflow Flexível foi realizada corretamente para o tipo de documento em questão;
  • Se existe alguma pendência na transação SWU3, garantindo que todos os itens estejam com o indicador em verde;
  • No aplicativo Administrar Workflows, se há pelo menos um Workflow ativo e se o documento em análise atende aos requisitos necessários para a inicialização.

 

3. Workflow não inicia

Verificar:

  • Se as condições de início do Workflow estão sendo atendidas (valor, tipo de documento, empresa, centro, etc.);
  • Se o documento está em status liberado para Workflow;
  • Se houve erro na criação dos eventos (via SWEL/SWELS);
  • Se o Workflow Flexível está ativo para o objeto específico.

 

Conclusão

Em resumo, a correta utilização das ferramentas e a análise estruturada dos erros são fundamentais para garantir o bom funcionamento do Workflow Flexível. Ao adotar uma abordagem organizada na investigação, é possível identificar as causas com mais agilidade e aplicar as correções de forma mais eficiente, contribuindo para a estabilidade dos processos no SAP.

entry.title: Re: inter company transfer of CWIP line items
entry.content: The recommended approach would be to use asset transfer postings for CWIP to maintain compliance. For POs, adopt the hybrid approach: close open POs in the transferor company and re‑issue them in the transferee company. This ensures all future deliveries and invoices flow into the surviving entity, keeping procurement and accounting clean.
